description: You are a researcher analyzing data from an experiment comparing the effectiveness of six insect sprays (Beall, 1942). The dataset InsectSprays.csv is loaded in jamovi with 72 observations. Variables: 'count' (number of insects surviving after spraying) and 'spray' (type of spray: A through F). Since the data consists of insect counts, which are non-negative integers with likely non-normal distributions, use non-parametric statistical methods to analyze the data. Perform descriptive statistics for 'count' split by 'spray', a Shapiro-Wilk test of normality on 'count', a Kruskal-Wallis test comparing 'count' across the six 'spray' groups, and pairwise comparisons to determine which specific spray pairs differ significantly. Save the completed analysis as /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/InsectSprayAnalysis.omv.
